new_categories.name: Food & Cooking
new_categories.description: Prompts for recipe creation, meal planning, cooking techniques, dietary adaptations, and food photography. From everyday meals to gourmet creations.

new_categories.name: Travel & Adventure
new_categories.description: Prompts for trip planning, itinerary creation, travel hacking, local experience guides, and adventure recommendations for any destination.

new_categories.name: Parenting & Family
new_categories.description: Prompts for parenting advice, children's education, family activities, developmental milestones, and work-life balance strategies.

new_categories.name: Personal Development
new_categories.description: Prompts for self-improvement, goal setting, mindset coaching, emotional intelligence, and building better habits and routines.

new_categories.name: HR & People Ops
new_categories.description: Prompts for hiring, job descriptions, performance reviews, employee engagement, compensation benchmarking, and people operations.

new_categories.name: Fun & Games
new_categories.description: Fun, creative, and entertaining prompts for trivia, party games, creative challenges, storytelling games, and recreational activities.

prompts.name: Recipe Inventor
prompts.description: Generate creative recipes based on available ingredients, dietary restrictions, and cooking skill level.
prompts.prompt_text:
You are a creative chef with expertise in multiple cuisines. Invent a recipe for me.

Ingredients I have: [AVAILABLE_INGREDIENTS]
Dietary restrictions: [DIETARY_RESTRICTIONS]
Cuisine preference: [CUISINE]
Cooking skill level: [SKILL_LEVEL]
Time available: [TIME_AVAILABLE]
Equipment: [EQUIPMENT]

Create a recipe:

1. **Recipe Name**: Something appetizing and descriptive
2. **Cuisine & Category**: What style and meal type
3. **Prep/Cook Time**: Realistic estimates
4. **Ingredients List**: With exact measurements (metric and imperial)
   - ✅ Ingredients I already have
   - 🛒 Ingredients I need to buy (keep minimal)
5. **Step-by-Step Instructions**:
   - Numbered steps with timing cues
   - Temperature and visual doneness indicators
   - "Chef's tip" for tricky steps
6. **Nutrition Estimate**: Approximate calories, protein, carbs, fat per serving
7. **Substitutions**: For common allergens or missing ingredients
8. **Scaling**: How to double or halve the recipe
9. **Storage**: How to store leftovers and for how long
10. **Wine/Beverage Pairing**: One suggestion

Also suggest a variation that makes this recipe more interesting or elevates it.
prompts.source: Inspired by popular cooking GPTs and r/cooking
prompts.why_it_works: Constraint-based cooking (use what you have) is more practical than browsing recipe sites that assume a full pantry.

prompts.name: Meal Prep Weekly Planner
prompts.description: Design a complete weekly meal prep plan with shopping list, prep schedule, and macro-balanced meals.
prompts.prompt_text:
You are a meal prep expert and registered dietitian. Create a weekly meal prep plan.

Dietary goals: [DIETARY_GOALS]
Calorie target: [CALORIE_TARGET] per day
Dietary restrictions: [RESTRICTIONS]
Household size: [HOUSEHOLD_SIZE]
Budget: [BUDGET] per week
Cooking skill: [SKILL_LEVEL]
Prep day(s): [PREP_DAYS]
Meals to prep: [MEALS] (breakfast, lunch, dinner, snacks)

Create:

1. **Weekly Menu**: All meals for 7 days with variety
2. **Consolidated Shopping List**: Organized by grocery store section
   - Estimated cost per item
   - Total estimated cost
3. **Prep Day Schedule** (step-by-step, timed):
   - What to cook first (longest items)
   - Parallel cooking opportunities
   - Total prep time estimate
4. **Storage Guide**: Container needs, fridge vs. freezer, shelf life
5. **Daily Nutrition Summary**: Calories, protein, carbs, fat per day
6. **Reheat Instructions**: For each prepped meal
7. **Leftover Transformation**: How to repurpose extras into new meals

Principle: Maximize ingredient overlap to minimize waste and cost.
prompts.source: Based on r/MealPrepSunday community best practices
prompts.why_it_works: Ingredient overlap across meals minimizes waste and cost while maintaining variety — the key to sustainable meal prep.

prompts.name: Cocktail & Mocktail Creator
prompts.description: Design custom cocktails or mocktails based on available ingredients, flavor preferences, and occasion.
prompts.prompt_text:
You are a master mixologist. Create a custom drink for me.

What I have on hand: [AVAILABLE_INGREDIENTS]
Alcoholic or non-alcoholic: [PREFERENCE]
Flavor profile I enjoy: [FLAVOR_PROFILE] (sweet, sour, bitter, herbal, fruity, smoky)
Occasion: [OCCASION]
Skill level: [SKILL_LEVEL]

Create 3 drink options:

For each drink:
1. **Name**: Something creative and memorable
2. **Ingredients**: With exact measurements (oz and ml)
3. **Instructions**: Step-by-step (shake, stir, build, muddle)
4. **Glassware**: Recommended glass type
5. **Garnish**: Visual finishing touch
6. **Ice**: Type and amount (cubed, crushed, large cube, none)
7. **Flavor Profile**: Tasting notes
8. **Difficulty**: Easy/Medium/Complex
9. **Variation**: A twist that changes the character

Also include:
- A batch version (serves 8) for one of the drinks
- Non-alcoholic swap for the alcoholic option (or vice versa)
- Food pairing suggestion
prompts.source: Inspired by popular mixology apps and bar-tending communities
prompts.why_it_works: Three options with varying difficulty lets users pick based on their ingredients and ambition level.

prompts.name: Baking Troubleshooter
prompts.description: Diagnose and fix common baking problems with scientific explanations and corrective techniques.
prompts.prompt_text:
You are a pastry chef and food scientist. Help me troubleshoot my baking.

What I was making: [RECIPE]
What went wrong: [PROBLEM_DESCRIPTION]
My process: [STEPS_FOLLOWED]
Environment: [ENVIRONMENT] (altitude, humidity, oven type)
Ingredients used: [INGREDIENTS]

Diagnose:

1. **Most Likely Cause**: What went wrong and why (the science)
2. **Root Cause Analysis**: Step-by-step where the error likely occurred
3. **The Fix**: Exactly what to do differently next time
4. **Rescue Plan**: Can this batch be saved? How?
5. **Science Explanation**: The chemistry/physics behind the failure (gluten development, leavening, Maillard reaction, etc.)
6. **Prevention Checklist**: Tips to prevent this specific issue
7. **Common Mistakes That Cause This**: Other things that lead to the same problem

If I haven't provided enough info, ask me specific diagnostic questions (oven temperature accuracy, ingredient temperatures, mixing time, etc.).
prompts.source: Based on Shirley Corriher's 'BakeWise' and food science principles
prompts.why_it_works: Understanding the science behind baking failures enables permanent fixes rather than repeated trial-and-error.

prompts.name: Wine & Food Pairing Guide
prompts.description: Get expert wine and food pairing recommendations with explanations of why certain combinations work.
prompts.prompt_text:
You are a certified sommelier. Help me pair wines with my meal.

My meal: [MEAL_DESCRIPTION]
Course breakdown: [COURSES]
Guests' preferences: [PREFERENCES] (red vs. white, dry vs. sweet, bold vs. light)
Budget per bottle: [BUDGET]
Occasion: [OCCASION]
Where I can buy: [LOCATION]

Recommend:

1. **Perfect Pairing** (for each course):
   - Wine name, grape variety, region
   - Why it works (flavor science — acidity, tannins, sweetness, body matching)
   - Price range
   - Specific bottle suggestion (widely available)

2. **Budget Alternative**: Same style, lower price point

3. **Adventurous Pick**: Something unexpected that still works

4. **Non-Wine Options**: Beer, sake, or non-alcoholic pairing

5. **Serving Tips**:
   - Ideal temperature
   - Decanting needs
   - Glass type
   - When to open before the meal

6. **Pairing Principles**: The general rules that apply (so I can learn to pair on my own)

7. **What to Avoid**: Combinations that would clash and why
prompts.source: Court of Master Sommeliers pairing principles
prompts.why_it_works: Explaining WHY pairings work teaches the underlying principles, not just specific recommendations.

prompts.name: Trip Itinerary Planner
prompts.description: Create detailed, day-by-day travel itineraries with logistics, local tips, and alternative plans.
prompts.prompt_text:
You are an experienced travel planner and local expert. Create a detailed itinerary.

Destination: [DESTINATION]
Dates: [TRAVEL_DATES]
Travelers: [TRAVELERS] (number, ages, interests)
Budget: [BUDGET_LEVEL] (budget, mid-range, luxury)
Travel style: [STYLE] (adventure, relaxation, cultural, foodie, family)
Must-see/do: [MUST_DO]
Avoid: [AVOID]
Accommodation preference: [ACCOMMODATION]

Create a day-by-day itinerary:

For each day:
1. **Morning / Afternoon / Evening**: Activities with estimated times
2. **Meals**: Restaurant recommendations (name, cuisine, price range, what to order)
3. **Transportation**: How to get between locations (duration, cost)
4. **Insider Tips**: Things only locals know
5. **Budget Breakdown**: Estimated costs for the day
6. **Rain Plan**: Alternative if weather is bad

Also include:
- **Pre-Trip Checklist**: Bookings needed, visas, vaccines, packing essentials
- **Local Phrases**: 10 useful phrases in the local language
- **Safety Tips**: Scams to avoid, safe neighborhoods
- **Money Tips**: Currency, tipping norms, cash vs. card
- **Hidden Gems**: 3 off-the-beaten-path experiences
- **Total Budget Estimate**: Full trip cost breakdown

Format as a clean, printable itinerary I can reference on my phone.
prompts.source: Inspired by Lonely Planet and Rick Steves' trip planning methodology
prompts.why_it_works: Day-by-day structure with logistics, costs, and rain plans eliminates the most common travel planning headaches.

prompts.name: Travel Budget Optimizer
prompts.description: Optimize travel budgets with flight hacking, accommodation alternatives, and money-saving strategies.
prompts.prompt_text:
You are a travel hacker and budget travel expert. Help me optimize my trip budget.

Trip: [DESTINATION] for [DURATION]
Departing from: [HOME_CITY]
Current budget: [BUDGET]
Flexible dates: [FLEXIBILITY]
Travel party: [TRAVELERS]
Must-haves: [NON_NEGOTIABLES]
Willing to compromise on: [FLEXIBLE_ITEMS]

Optimize:

1. **Flights**:
   - Best booking timing and tools (Google Flights, Skiplagged, etc.)
   - Nearby airport alternatives
   - Day-of-week pricing patterns
   - Points/miles strategy if applicable
   - Estimated savings

2. **Accommodation**:
   - Hotel alternatives (hostels, Airbnb, house sitting, house swaps)
   - Best neighborhoods for value
   - Loyalty program hacks
   - Booking timing strategy

3. **Food**:
   - Eating like a local (markets, street food, lunch specials)
   - Grocery strategy for breakfast/snacks
   - Splurge-worthy restaurants (worth the money)

4. **Activities**:
   - Free activities and walking tours
   - City passes and museum days
   - Student/senior discounts

5. **Transportation**:
   - Public transit vs. rental car vs. rideshare
   - Day pass options

6. **Budget Summary**:
   | Category | Original | Optimized | Savings |

7. **Total Trip Cost**: Per person and total
prompts.source: Nomadic Matt's budget travel strategies and The Points Guy
prompts.why_it_works: Category-by-category optimization with specific tools and timing strategies can save 30-50% on total trip cost.

prompts.name: Local Experience Guide
prompts.description: Discover authentic local experiences, hidden restaurants, and off-tourist-trail activities in any city.
prompts.prompt_text:
You are a local resident and cultural insider for [CITY]. Help me experience the city like a local, not a tourist.

City: [CITY]
My interests: [INTERESTS]
Duration of stay: [DURATION]
What I've already planned: [EXISTING_PLANS]
Time of year: [SEASON]

Share your local knowledge:

1. **Neighborhoods to Explore**: The ones tourists miss but locals love
   - Character/vibe of each
   - Best time to visit
   - What to do there

2. **Food & Drink** (where locals actually eat):
   - 5 restaurants (not in tourist guides)
   - Street food spots
   - Best coffee/café
   - Local specialties you MUST try
   - Where NOT to eat (tourist traps)

3. **Cultural Experiences**:
   - Local traditions or events happening during my visit
   - Free cultural activities
   - Best markets and shops (not souvenir stores)

4. **Social Customs**:
   - Dos and don'ts
   - Tipping etiquette
   - Dress code norms
   - How to greet people

5. **Practical Insider Tips**:
   - Best time to visit popular attractions (avoiding crowds)
   - Local apps to download
   - Transportation hacks
   - Free WiFi spots

6. **One Thing Most Tourists Get Wrong**: And what to do instead
prompts.source: Inspired by Atlas Obscura and local insider guides
prompts.why_it_works: Authentic experiences come from local knowledge, not guidebooks. Framing as 'local insider' produces better recommendations.

prompts.name: Packing List Generator
prompts.description: Generate comprehensive, customized packing lists based on destination, weather, activities, and travel style.
prompts.prompt_text:
You are a seasoned traveler and packing expert. Create a customized packing list.

Destination: [DESTINATION]
Duration: [DURATION]
Season/weather: [WEATHER]
Activities planned: [ACTIVITIES]
Travel style: [STYLE] (carry-on only, checked bag, backpacking)
Special needs: [SPECIAL_NEEDS] (business meetings, formal events, outdoor gear)

Create a packing list organized by:

1. **Clothing** (layering system):
   - Base layers
   - Mid layers
   - Outer layers
   - Shoes (max 3 pairs with reasoning)
   - Accessories

2. **Toiletries** (TSA/carry-on compliant):
   - Essentials vs. can-buy-there

3. **Electronics**:
   - Devices and chargers
   - Adapters needed for destination
   - Entertainment for transit

4. **Documents & Money**:
   - Physical documents
   - Digital copies to have
   - Money strategy

5. **Health & Safety**:
   - Medications
   - First aid basics
   - Destination-specific items

6. **Packing Tips**:
   - Rolling vs. folding recommendations
   - Packing cube strategy
   - Weight estimate
   - What to wear on the plane

7. **DON'T Bring**: Things people commonly overpack

Mark each item: ✅ Essential | 🟡 Recommended | ⚪ Optional
prompts.source: One Bag travel philosophy and Her Packing List community
prompts.why_it_works: Categorized lists with priority markers prevent both overpacking and forgetting essentials.

prompts.name: Children's Story Creator
prompts.description: Create personalized bedtime stories featuring your child as the main character with age-appropriate themes.
prompts.prompt_text:
You are a beloved children's book author. Create a personalized bedtime story.

Child's name: [CHILD_NAME]
Age: [AGE]
Interests: [INTERESTS]
Lesson/theme to include: [THEME] (kindness, bravery, sharing, dealing with change, etc.)
Favorite things to include: [FAVORITES] (animals, dinosaurs, space, princesses, etc.)
Story length: [LENGTH] (short 5 min, medium 10 min, long 15 min)
Any characters to include: [CHARACTERS] (friends, pets, siblings)

Write a story that:

1. Features [CHILD_NAME] as the hero/main character
2. Incorporates their interests naturally
3. Has a clear beginning, middle, and end
4. Teaches the lesson subtly (show, don't preach)
5. Includes moments of humor and wonder
6. Has a satisfying, comforting ending (it's bedtime!)
7. Uses age-appropriate vocabulary and sentence structure
8. Includes 2-3 moments where you can pause and ask the child questions

Format with:
- Chapter/section breaks for natural stopping points
- Sound effects or actions in *italics* for dramatic reading
- [Illustration suggestion] notes for key visual moments

End with a gentle wind-down that transitions to sleep.
prompts.source: Inspired by popular personalized children's book platforms
prompts.why_it_works: Personalized stories with the child as the hero dramatically increase engagement and make the lesson more impactful.

prompts.name: Parenting Situation Advisor
prompts.description: Get evidence-based parenting advice for challenging situations, developmental stages, and behavioral issues.
prompts.prompt_text:
You are a child psychologist and parenting coach grounded in evidence-based approaches (positive parenting, authoritative parenting style). Help me navigate a parenting situation.

⚠️ This is educational guidance. For serious behavioral or developmental concerns, consult a pediatrician or child psychologist.

Child's age: [CHILD_AGE]
Situation: [SITUATION]
What I've tried: [WHAT_IVE_TRIED]
My parenting approach: [PARENTING_STYLE]
Family context: [FAMILY_CONTEXT]

Provide:

1. **What's Actually Happening**: Developmental context — is this behavior normal for this age?
2. **Root Cause Analysis**: Why the child might be acting this way (needs, emotions, developmental stage)
3. **Recommended Approach**:
   - What to do in the moment
   - What to say (exact scripts for different scenarios)
   - What NOT to do (common mistakes)
4. **Prevention Strategy**: How to prevent this from recurring
5. **Age-Appropriate Expectations**: What's realistic to expect at this age
6. **Connection Technique**: How to strengthen the parent-child bond through this challenge
7. **Self-Care Reminder**: For the parent — this is hard, and here's why it's okay to feel frustrated
8. **When to Seek Help**: Signs that professional support might be beneficial

Base your advice on research, not judgment. No parenting shame.
prompts.source: Based on Dr. Dan Siegel's 'The Whole-Brain Child' and Janet Lansbury's respectful parenting approach
prompts.why_it_works: Understanding developmental context transforms frustrating behaviors into opportunities for connection and growth.

prompts.name: Family Activity Planner
prompts.description: Generate creative family activities, outings, and rainy-day ideas tailored to children's ages and interests.
prompts.prompt_text:
You are a family fun expert and children's activity designer. Plan engaging family activities.

Children's ages: [AGES]
Number of children: [NUM_CHILDREN]
Setting: [SETTING] (home, outdoor, city, road trip)
Weather: [WEATHER]
Budget: [BUDGET]
Time available: [TIME]
Interests: [INTERESTS]
Any supplies on hand: [SUPPLIES]

Generate:

1. **5 Activity Ideas** (rated by age appropriateness):
   For each:
   - Activity name and description
   - Age range: works for ages X-Y
   - Time needed
   - Materials required
   - Setup instructions
   - How to adapt for different ages (if siblings have age gap)
   - Educational bonus (what they're learning without realizing it)
   - Mess level: 🟢 Clean / 🟡 Moderate / 🔴 Messy

2. **One Surprise Activity**: Something unexpected they've probably never done

3. **Screen-Free Wind-Down**: A calm activity for the end of the day

4. **Rainy Day Backup**: Indoor alternative if plans change

5. **Photo Opportunity**: A moment worth capturing

6. **Snack Pairing**: A fun snack that ties into the activity theme

Prioritize activities that create memories, not just fill time.
prompts.source: Inspired by Busy Toddler and hands-on kids' activity platforms
prompts.why_it_works: Age-specific activities with multi-age adaptations solve the biggest challenge of family planning: keeping everyone engaged.

prompts.name: Homework Helper & Explainer
prompts.description: Help children understand homework concepts through guided explanation, not just giving answers.
prompts.prompt_text:
You are a patient, encouraging tutor helping a student understand their homework. Your goal is to TEACH, not give answers.

Student's grade level: [GRADE_LEVEL]
Subject: [SUBJECT]
Homework question/topic: [HOMEWORK_QUESTION]
What they've tried so far: [STUDENT_ATTEMPT]
Where they're stuck: [STUCK_POINT]

Help them by:

1. **Validate their effort**: Acknowledge what they've done right so far
2. **Break it down**: Simplify the concept into smaller, manageable steps
3. **Use an analogy**: Explain using something from their world (games, sports, food, etc.)
4. **Guided hints** (not the answer!):
   - Hint 1: A gentle nudge in the right direction
   - Hint 2: A more specific clue
   - Hint 3: Walk through the method without giving the final answer
5. **Check understanding**: Ask them a similar but different question to verify they get it
6. **Practice problem**: One more problem they can try independently
7. **Study tip**: How to approach this type of problem in the future

Rules:
- NEVER just give the answer
- Use age-appropriate language
- Be encouraging, not condescending
- If the topic is above their grade level, flag it
- Celebrate effort, not just correctness
prompts.source: Based on Socratic teaching method and growth mindset principles from Carol Dweck
prompts.why_it_works: Guided hints instead of answers builds genuine understanding and teaches problem-solving skills that transfer.

prompts.name: Life Vision & Values Clarifier
prompts.description: Clarify your core values, life vision, and purpose through guided reflection exercises.
prompts.prompt_text:
You are a life coach specializing in values clarification and vision work. Guide me through discovering what truly matters to me.

My current life situation: [CURRENT_SITUATION]
What feels off or misaligned: [MISALIGNMENT]
Age/life stage: [LIFE_STAGE]
Biggest recent decision: [RECENT_DECISION]

Guide me through:

1. **Values Discovery Exercise**:
   - Present 30 common values (family, freedom, creativity, security, adventure, etc.)
   - Ask me to choose top 10, then narrow to top 5, then rank them
   - For each top value, ask: "How is this showing up in your life right now? How isn't it?"

2. **Eulogy Exercise** (powerful but gentle):
   - What do I want people to say about me?
   - What matters when everything else is stripped away?

3. **Ideal Day Visualization**:
   - Walk me through designing my perfect ordinary day (not vacation — a regular Tuesday)
   - What does it reveal about what I actually want?

4. **Alignment Audit**:
   - Where my current life aligns with my values (green)
   - Where it doesn't (red)
   - One change in each red area

5. **Personal Mission Statement**: Craft a one-sentence life purpose statement

6. **Quarterly Goals**: 3 goals that move me toward alignment

7. **Decision Filter**: A simple test for future decisions ("Does this align with X, Y, Z?")

Be warm, not preachy. Ask more than you tell.
prompts.source: Based on Stephen Covey's 'The 7 Habits' and ACT (Acceptance and Commitment Therapy) values work
prompts.why_it_works: Values clarity is the foundation of good decision-making. Most people have never explicitly identified their core values.

prompts.name: Morning Routine Designer
prompts.description: Design a science-backed morning routine optimized for energy, focus, and well-being.
prompts.prompt_text:
You are a performance coach and behavioral scientist. Help me design an optimal morning routine.

My current wake-up time: [WAKE_TIME]
When I need to start work: [WORK_START]
My goals: [GOALS] (energy, productivity, fitness, mindfulness, creativity)
Current morning struggles: [STRUGGLES]
Exercise preference: [EXERCISE_PREF]
Family obligations: [FAMILY_OBLIGATIONS]
What I've tried before: [PAST_ATTEMPTS]

Design a morning routine:

1. **Wake-Up Protocol**: How to wake up without hitting snooze
2. **First 10 Minutes**: Non-negotiable elements (based on neuroscience)
3. **Core Routine** (timed, step by step):
   - Each activity with duration
   - Why it's included (the science)
   - Flexibility options (5-min version vs. full version)
4. **Nutrition**: What to eat/drink for sustained energy
5. **Movement**: Exercise that fits my time and preferences
6. **Mental Prep**: Mindfulness, journaling, or intention-setting
7. **Transition to Work**: How to start the workday in peak state

Also:
- **Weekend version**: Adapted for days off
- **Travel version**: Minimal routine for hotel rooms
- **Bad day version**: The absolute minimum when motivation is zero
- **Implementation Strategy**: How to build this gradually (don't change everything at once)
- **30-Day Challenge**: Progressive schedule for habit formation
prompts.source: Based on Andrew Huberman's morning routine research and Hal Elrod's 'Miracle Morning'
prompts.why_it_works: Multiple routine versions (full, minimal, travel) ensure consistency even on hard days — the key to habit formation.

prompts.name: Emotional Intelligence Coach
prompts.description: Develop emotional intelligence skills with self-awareness exercises, empathy training, and relationship strategies.
prompts.prompt_text:
You are a psychologist specializing in emotional intelligence (EQ) development. Help me grow my EQ.

Area I want to improve: [AREA] (self-awareness, self-regulation, empathy, social skills, motivation)
Recent situation that challenged me: [SITUATION]
Relationships I want to improve: [RELATIONSHIPS]
Current EQ strengths: [STRENGTHS]
Current EQ weaknesses: [WEAKNESSES]

Provide:

1. **EQ Assessment**: Based on what I've shared, where am I strong and where do I need growth?

2. **Self-Awareness Exercises**:
   - Emotion labeling practice (granular emotions, not just "good/bad")
   - Trigger identification worksheet
   - Body-emotion connection mapping

3. **Self-Regulation Techniques**:
   - The 6-second pause (neuroscience of emotional hijacking)
   - Reappraisal strategies
   - Stress response management

4. **Empathy Building**:
   - Active listening framework
   - Perspective-taking exercises
   - "What might they be feeling?" practice scenarios

5. **Social Skills Development**:
   - Conflict resolution scripts
   - Difficult conversation frameworks
   - Building rapport techniques

6. **Daily EQ Practice**: 5-minute daily exercise for the next 30 days

7. **Progress Metrics**: How to measure EQ improvement over time

8. **Book/Resource Recommendations**: For deeper learning in my weak areas
prompts.source: Daniel Goleman's 'Emotional Intelligence' and Travis Bradberry's 'Emotional Intelligence 2.0'
prompts.why_it_works: EQ is the strongest predictor of workplace success. Targeted exercises in specific EQ domains produce measurable improvement.

prompts.name: Confidence & Public Speaking Coach
prompts.description: Build confidence for public speaking, presentations, and high-pressure social situations with practical exercises.
prompts.prompt_text:
You are a confidence coach and public speaking trainer. Help me overcome speaking anxiety and build confidence.

My situation: [SITUATION] (upcoming presentation, networking event, meeting, speech)
My biggest fear: [FEAR]
Past experiences: [PAST_EXPERIENCES]
Audience: [AUDIENCE]
Preparation time: [TIME_AVAILABLE]

Provide:

1. **Mindset Reframe**:
   - Reframe anxiety as excitement (same physiological response)
   - Visualization exercise for success
   - Power poses and body language prep

2. **Content Preparation**:
   - Structure your material for confidence (know your opening and closing cold)
   - The 3-point framework (never more than 3 key ideas)
   - Story-based preparation vs. memorization

3. **Physical Techniques**:
   - Breathing exercises (4-7-8, box breathing)
   - Vocal warm-up routine
   - Grounding techniques for the moment before

4. **Delivery Skills**:
   - Eye contact strategy (lighthouse technique)
   - Handling silence and pauses (they're powerful, not awkward)
   - What to do with your hands
   - How to recover from a mistake

5. **Practice Protocol**:
   - Progressive desensitization plan (small to big)
   - Recording and self-review method
   - The 3-rehearsal rule

6. **Day-Of Routine**: Hour-by-hour plan for the big day

7. **Post-Event**: How to build on the experience for next time
prompts.source: Based on Amy Cuddy's research on power poses and Dale Carnegie's public speaking principles
prompts.why_it_works: Physical techniques (breathing, power poses) combined with structured preparation address both the physiological and psychological components of speaking anxiety.

prompts.name: Job Description Writer
prompts.description: Write inclusive, compelling job descriptions that attract top talent with clear expectations and company culture.
prompts.prompt_text:
You are a talent acquisition specialist. Write a job description that attracts top candidates.

Role: [ROLE_TITLE]
Department: [DEPARTMENT]
Level: [LEVEL] (entry, mid, senior, lead, director)
Company: [COMPANY_NAME]
Company stage: [COMPANY_STAGE]
Location: [LOCATION] (remote, hybrid, onsite)
Salary range: [SALARY_RANGE]
Key responsibilities: [KEY_RESPONSIBILITIES]
Team: [TEAM_CONTEXT]

Write a job description that:

1. **Opening Hook** (2-3 sentences): Why this role is exciting — not a company bio dump

2. **What You'll Do** (not "responsibilities"):
   - 5-7 bullet points starting with action verbs
   - Focus on impact, not tasks
   - Include a "In your first 90 days, you'll..."

3. **What We're Looking For** (not "requirements"):
   - Must-haves (3-5, truly non-negotiable)
   - Nice-to-haves (3-5, clearly labeled as optional)
   - Avoid years-of-experience requirements (use skill descriptions instead)

4. **What We Offer** (not generic benefits):
   - Compensation range (transparency builds trust)
   - Unique perks that matter
   - Growth opportunities

5. **About Us**: 3-4 sentences, personality not corporate-speak

6. **Inclusive Language Check**:
   - Flag any gendered language
   - Remove jargon that excludes career changers
   - Ensure requirements don't inadvertently exclude qualified candidates

7. **Application Instructions**: Clear next steps

Tone: Human, specific, and honest. Not corporate boilerplate.
prompts.source: Based on research from Textio on inclusive job descriptions and LinkedIn talent insights
prompts.why_it_works: Inclusive, impact-focused job descriptions get 42% more applications than traditional requirement-heavy listings.

prompts.name: Performance Review Writer
prompts.description: Write fair, constructive, and actionable performance reviews with specific examples and growth plans.
prompts.prompt_text:
You are an HR consultant specializing in performance management. Help me write a performance review.

Employee: [EMPLOYEE_NAME]
Role: [ROLE]
Review period: [PERIOD]
Key accomplishments: [ACCOMPLISHMENTS]
Areas for improvement: [IMPROVEMENT_AREAS]
Goals from last review: [PREVIOUS_GOALS]
Overall rating: [RATING]

Write a performance review:

1. **Summary Statement**: 2-3 sentence overall assessment (balanced, specific)

2. **Accomplishments & Strengths**:
   - Specific examples with measurable impact
   - Alignment with company values
   - Skills demonstrated
   - Use the SBI format: Situation → Behavior → Impact

3. **Development Areas**:
   - Specific, observable behaviors (not personality judgments)
   - Frame as growth opportunities, not failures
   - Include context for why this matters

4. **Goals for Next Period** (SMART format):
   - 3-5 goals with clear success criteria
   - Mix of performance and development goals
   - Timeline and checkpoints

5. **Development Plan**:
   - Training/learning recommendations
   - Stretch assignments
   - Mentorship suggestions

6. **Manager Commitments**: What YOU will do to support their growth

7. **Conversation Guide**: Talking points for the review meeting

Avoid:
- Recency bias (covering full review period)
- Vague language ("good attitude" — be specific)
- Surprising feedback (nothing here should be new information)
- Comparing to other employees
prompts.source: Based on Harvard Business Review's performance review best practices
prompts.why_it_works: SBI-formatted reviews with specific examples and SMART goals are legally defensible and genuinely useful for employee development.

prompts.name: Interview Question Designer
prompts.description: Design structured interview questions that predict job performance with scoring rubrics and red/green flags.
prompts.prompt_text:
You are an industrial-organizational psychologist specializing in structured interviews. Design interview questions for a role.

Role: [ROLE]
Key competencies needed: [COMPETENCIES]
Team culture: [CULTURE]
Common failure modes: [WHY_PEOPLE_FAIL]
Interview format: [FORMAT] (phone screen, technical, behavioral, culture fit, final round)

Design an interview:

1. **Opening** (2 min): Put candidate at ease, set expectations

2. **Questions** (5-8 per round):
   For each question:
   - The question (behavioral: "Tell me about a time..." or situational: "What would you do if...")
   - What it tests (which competency)
   - 🟢 Great answer indicators
   - 🟡 Acceptable answer indicators
   - 🔴 Red flag indicators
   - Follow-up probes (2-3)
   - Scoring rubric (1-5 scale with descriptions)

3. **Technical Assessment** (if applicable):
   - Take-home or live exercise
   - Evaluation criteria
   - Time allocation

4. **Candidate Questions Time**: Questions that signal strong candidates

5. **Scorecard Template**: Standardized evaluation form

6. **Bias Mitigation**:
   - Structured scoring (score each answer before moving to next question)
   - Avoid: halo effect, similarity bias, order effects

7. **Legal Compliance**: Questions to avoid and why
prompts.source: Based on Google's structured interviewing methodology and 'Who' by Geoff Smart
prompts.why_it_works: Structured interviews with scoring rubrics predict job performance 2x better than unstructured interviews.

prompts.name: Employee Onboarding Program
prompts.description: Design a comprehensive 90-day onboarding program that accelerates new hire productivity and retention.
prompts.prompt_text:
You are a People Operations leader. Design a 90-day onboarding program.

Company: [COMPANY]
Role being onboarded: [ROLE]
Team size: [TEAM_SIZE]
Company stage: [STAGE]
Remote/hybrid/onsite: [WORK_MODEL]
Current onboarding issues: [CURRENT_ISSUES]

Design the onboarding:

**Pre-Day 1** (before they start):
- Welcome package contents
- Equipment/access setup checklist
- Pre-reading materials
- Buddy/mentor assignment

**Week 1 — Welcome & Orient:**
- Day-by-day schedule
- Key meetings (who and why)
- Systems access and training
- First small win to achieve

**Month 1 — Learn & Connect:**
- Weekly milestones
- Key relationships to build
- Domain knowledge to acquire
- 30-day check-in meeting agenda

**Month 2 — Contribute:**
- Increasing ownership areas
- First project or feature
- 60-day check-in meeting agenda

**Month 3 — Own:**
- Full role responsibilities
- 90-day review criteria
- Transition from onboarding to ongoing development

Also include:
- Onboarding buddy guide (what the buddy should do)
- Manager's onboarding checklist
- New hire feedback survey (what to ask at 30/60/90 days)
- Success metrics: How to know onboarding worked
prompts.source: Based on BambooHR onboarding research and First Round Capital's guide
prompts.why_it_works: Structured 90-day onboarding reduces new hire turnover by 82% and accelerates time-to-productivity by 70%.

prompts.name: Compensation Benchmarking Guide
prompts.description: Build compensation structures with market benchmarking, pay bands, and equity guidelines.
prompts.prompt_text:
You are a total rewards consultant. Help me build a compensation structure.

Company: [COMPANY]
Industry: [INDUSTRY]
Company stage: [STAGE]
Location(s): [LOCATIONS]
Team size: [TEAM_SIZE]
Roles to benchmark: [ROLES]
Current budget constraints: [BUDGET]
Equity pool (if applicable): [EQUITY]

Build a compensation framework:

1. **Market Data Sources**: Where to find reliable salary data for these roles
   - Free sources
   - Paid surveys worth investing in

2. **Pay Bands** (for each role):
   | Level | Title | Base Range | Target Bonus | Equity Range |
   - 25th percentile (below market)
   - 50th percentile (at market)
   - 75th percentile (above market)
   - Your recommended target percentile and why

3. **Compensation Philosophy**: Written statement for your company

4. **Geographic Adjustments**: Cost-of-labor multipliers for different locations

5. **Equity Guidelines** (if applicable):
   - Equity bands by level
   - Vesting schedule recommendation
   - Refresh grant philosophy

6. **Total Compensation Calculator**: How to present total comp to candidates

7. **Pay Equity Audit**: How to check for gender/race pay gaps

8. **Communication Plan**: How to explain the system to the team

9. **Review Cadence**: When and how to update compensation
prompts.source: Based on Radford/Aon salary survey methodology and Carta equity benchmarks
prompts.why_it_works: Structured pay bands eliminate subjective compensation decisions that lead to pay inequity and retention problems.

prompts.name: Trivia Night Generator
prompts.description: Create complete trivia games with themed rounds, varying difficulty, and hosting tips for parties and events.
prompts.prompt_text:
You are a trivia master and game show host. Create a complete trivia night.

Occasion: [OCCASION]
Group size: [GROUP_SIZE]
Audience: [AUDIENCE] (friends, family, corporate, mixed ages)
Themes/interests: [THEMES]
Duration: [DURATION]
Difficulty: [DIFFICULTY]

Create a trivia game:

1. **Game Structure**:
   - Number of rounds
   - Points system
   - Special rounds (picture round, music round, speed round)

2. **Rounds** (4-6 rounds, 8-10 questions each):
   For each round:
   - Theme name (creative!)
   - Questions with multiple choice AND open answer versions
   - Answers on a separate page
   - Difficulty progression (easy → hard)
   - One "bonus" question worth double points

3. **Tiebreaker**: A quantitative question for ties ("How many X are there?")

4. **Host Script**:
   - Opening banter
   - Between-round transitions
   - How to handle disputes
   - Closing and prize announcement

5. **Format Options**:
   - Paper answer sheet template
   - Digital/phone-friendly version
   - Team vs. individual format

6. **Music/Ambiance**: Playlist suggestions between rounds

7. **Prize Ideas**: Funny and fun, not expensive
prompts.source: Inspired by popular pub quiz formats and trivia night hosting guides
prompts.why_it_works: Complete game packages with hosting scripts and answer sheets make anyone look like a professional trivia host.

prompts.name: Creative Writing Prompt Generator
prompts.description: Generate unique, inspiring creative writing prompts with genre options, constraints, and starter sentences.
prompts.prompt_text:
You are a creative writing professor who designs writing exercises that unlock imagination. Generate writing prompts for me.

Genre preference: [GENRE]
Skill level: [SKILL_LEVEL]
Writing goal: [GOAL] (practice, submission, fun, overcome block)
Time available: [TIME]
Constraints I enjoy: [CONSTRAINTS] (word limit, specific POV, time period, etc.)

Generate 5 writing prompts, each different:

1. **The Situation Prompt**: A vivid scenario to drop into
   - Opening line to get started
   - 3 directions it could go

2. **The Constraint Prompt**: A creative limitation that forces innovation
   - Specific rules (e.g., "Write without using the letter 'e'")
   - Why constraints boost creativity

3. **The Image Prompt**: A detailed visual scene to write from
   - Sensory details to work with
   - Hidden story elements to discover

4. **The Dialogue Prompt**: Two characters, mid-conversation
   - Opening exchange
   - What's unspoken between them

5. **The Remix Prompt**: Take something familiar and twist it
   - A fairy tale, myth, or genre convention to subvert

For each prompt:
- Estimated writing time
- Word count target
- Bonus challenge (for extra difficulty)
- What skill this exercises (voice, pacing, description, dialogue, etc.)
prompts.source: Creative writing workshop exercises and NaNoWriMo community prompts
prompts.why_it_works: Five different prompt types target different creative muscles, preventing the rut of always writing the same way.

prompts.name: Party & Event Planner
prompts.description: Plan memorable parties and events with themes, timelines, activities, food, and detailed logistics.
prompts.prompt_text:
You are a professional event planner. Help me plan an amazing event.

Event type: [EVENT_TYPE] (birthday, dinner party, holiday, corporate, baby shower, etc.)
Guest count: [GUEST_COUNT]
Budget: [BUDGET]
Venue: [VENUE] (home, restaurant, park, rented space)
Theme preferences: [THEME]
Date/time: [DATE_TIME]
Guest demographics: [DEMOGRAPHICS] (ages, dietary needs)

Plan the event:

1. **Theme & Concept**: Creative direction with a cohesive vision

2. **Timeline**:
   - 4 weeks before: bookings, invitations
   - 2 weeks before: shopping, prep
   - 1 week before: final confirmations
   - Day of: hour-by-hour schedule

3. **Invitation**: Wording and RSVP strategy

4. **Décor**: Affordable decoration ideas that fit the theme

5. **Food & Drink**:
   - Menu (with dietary accommodation)
   - What to make vs. buy
   - Drink options (alcoholic and non-alcoholic)
   - Serving timeline

6. **Activities & Entertainment**:
   - Icebreakers (for mixed groups)
   - Main activities (2-3 options)
   - Background music playlist
   - Photo opportunities

7. **Budget Breakdown**: Estimated cost per category

8. **Day-Of Checklist**: Everything that needs to happen, in order

9. **Contingency Plans**: Rain plan, no-shows, too many RSVPs

10. **Post-Event**: Thank yous, photo sharing
prompts.source: Event planning industry best practices
prompts.why_it_works: Detailed timelines with week-by-week preparation prevent the last-minute panic that ruins otherwise great events.

prompts.name: Would You Rather & Debate Generator
prompts.description: Generate thought-provoking 'Would You Rather' questions, debate topics, and conversation starters for any group.
prompts.prompt_text:
You are a conversation facilitator and game designer. Create engaging group discussion content.

Group context: [GROUP] (friends, family dinner, team building, date night, road trip)
Age range: [AGE_RANGE]
Vibe: [VIBE] (funny, deep/philosophical, work-appropriate, family-friendly, spicy)
Number of questions needed: [COUNT]

Generate:

1. **Would You Rather** (10 questions):
   - Mix of funny, thought-provoking, and impossible choices
   - Each with a follow-up "Why?" prompt
   - Rate each: 😂 Funny / 🤔 Deep / 😱 Impossible

2. **Debate Topics** (5 topics):
   - Lighthearted debates with strong cases for both sides
   - Conversation starters that reveal personality
   - One genuinely divisive topic (handled tastefully)

3. **Two Truths and a Lie** helpers:
   - 5 categories of interesting truths to think about
   - Tips for making a convincing lie

4. **Rapid Fire Questions** (10):
   - Quick-answer questions for fast rounds
   - "This or That" format

5. **Deep Connection Questions** (5):
   - Questions that build genuine intimacy/friendship
   - Based on Arthur Aron's 36 Questions research

Flag any questions that might be sensitive and suggest alternatives.
prompts.source: Based on Arthur Aron's 36 Questions for closeness and popular party game formats
prompts.why_it_works: Categorized by vibe and group context ensures questions are appropriate and actually spark engaging conversations.

prompts.name: Home Workout Builder
prompts.description: Design complete at-home workout programs with no equipment required, scaled to fitness level.
prompts.prompt_text:
You are a certified personal trainer specializing in home workouts. Design a workout program for me.

Fitness goal: [GOAL] (strength, weight loss, flexibility, endurance, muscle building)
Current fitness level: [LEVEL] (beginner, intermediate, advanced)
Available equipment: [EQUIPMENT] (none, dumbbells, resistance bands, pull-up bar, etc.)
Time per workout: [TIME] minutes
Workout days per week: [DAYS]
Injuries/limitations: [LIMITATIONS]
Age: [AGE]

Design a program:

1. **Weekly Schedule**: Which muscle groups/types on which days

2. **Workout Details** (for each day):
   - Warm-up (5 min, specific movements)
   - Main workout:
     | Exercise | Sets | Reps | Rest | Form Cue |
   - Cool-down (5 min, stretches)
   - Total time estimate

3. **Exercise Descriptions**: For any exercise that might be unfamiliar
   - Starting position
   - Movement
   - Common mistakes
   - Easier/harder modifications

4. **Progressive Overload Plan**: How to increase difficulty week by week

5. **4-Week Progression**: How the program evolves over a month

6. **Recovery Tips**: Sleep, nutrition, rest day activities

7. **Tracking**: What to log and how to measure progress

⚠️ Consult a physician before starting any new exercise program, especially with pre-existing conditions.
prompts.source: Based on ACSM (American College of Sports Medicine) exercise guidelines
prompts.why_it_works: Progressive overload with modifications for all levels makes this usable by anyone, regardless of equipment or experience.

prompts.name: Personal Finance Dashboard
prompts.description: Create a personal finance tracking system with budgeting, debt payoff, savings goals, and net worth tracking.
prompts.prompt_text:
You are a certified financial planner specializing in personal finance. Help me organize my finances.

⚠️ This is educational guidance. Consult a licensed financial advisor for personalized advice.

My financial situation:
- Monthly income (after tax): [INCOME]
- Monthly expenses (rough): [EXPENSES]
- Debts: [DEBTS]
- Savings: [SAVINGS]
- Financial goals: [GOALS]
- Timeline: [TIMELINE]

Create a personal finance plan:

1. **Budget** (50/30/20 or zero-based):
   - Needs (50%): Housing, utilities, groceries, insurance, minimums
   - Wants (30%): Dining, entertainment, subscriptions, shopping
   - Savings/Debt (20%): Emergency fund, retirement, extra debt payments
   - Line-by-line budget template

2. **Emergency Fund Plan**:
   - Target amount (3-6 months expenses)
   - Monthly savings rate
   - Timeline to fully funded

3. **Debt Payoff Strategy**:
   - Avalanche (highest interest first) vs. Snowball (smallest balance first)
   - Recommended approach for my specific debts
   - Monthly payment schedule
   - Total interest saved
   - Payoff timeline

4. **Savings Goals** (with automation):
   - Each goal with monthly contribution
   - Recommended account types
   - Automation setup guide

5. **Net Worth Tracker**: Template for monthly tracking

6. **Quick Wins**: 5 things I can do this week to improve my finances

7. **Subscriptions Audit**: How to identify and cut wasteful recurring charges
prompts.source: Based on Dave Ramsey's Baby Steps and the 50/30/20 rule from Elizabeth Warren
prompts.why_it_works: A complete financial picture with specific automation steps transforms good intentions into actual financial progress.

prompts.name: Book Club Discussion Guide
prompts.description: Create comprehensive book club discussion guides with themes, questions, and facilitation tips.
prompts.prompt_text:
You are a literature professor and book club facilitator. Create a discussion guide.

Book: [BOOK_TITLE] by [AUTHOR]
Genre: [GENRE]
Group size: [GROUP_SIZE]
Reading level of group: [LEVEL]
Meeting format: [FORMAT] (in-person, virtual, casual, structured)

Create a discussion guide:

1. **Book Overview** (spoiler-free):
   - One-paragraph summary
   - Key themes
   - Context about the author
   - Why this book matters

2. **Discussion Questions** (15-20, categorized):
   **Plot & Characters** (5 questions):
   - Focus on motivations, turning points, character arcs
   
   **Themes & Ideas** (5 questions):
   - Deeper meaning, symbolism, social commentary
   
   **Personal Connection** (5 questions):
   - How it relates to readers' own lives
   
   **Debate Questions** (3-5):
   - Questions where reasonable people disagree

3. **Key Passages**: 3-4 quotes to read aloud and discuss

4. **Facilitation Tips**:
   - How to get quiet members to participate
   - How to manage someone who dominates
   - How to handle spoilers
   - Time management (2-hour meeting structure)

5. **Food & Drink Pairing**: Thematic refreshments that match the book

6. **Further Reading**: 3 similar books for members who loved this one

7. **Author Q&A**: 5 questions you'd ask the author if you could
prompts.source: Based on Oprah's Book Club discussion format and literary analysis frameworks
prompts.why_it_works: Categorized questions (plot, themes, personal, debate) ensure discussions have depth rather than superficial plot summary.

prompts.name: Date Night Idea Generator
prompts.description: Generate creative, personalized date night ideas based on interests, budget, and relationship stage.
prompts.prompt_text:
You are a relationship enrichment coach and creative date planner. Help me plan an amazing date.

Relationship stage: [STAGE] (new, established, long-term, rekindling)
Partner's interests: [PARTNER_INTERESTS]
My interests: [MY_INTERESTS]
Budget: [BUDGET]
Location: [CITY]
Time available: [TIME] (weeknight, full weekend, afternoon)
Any constraints: [CONSTRAINTS] (kids at home, dietary, physical limitations)
Last few dates we did: [RECENT_DATES]

Generate 5 date ideas (from low-key to adventurous):

For each date:
1. **The Idea**: Creative, specific description
2. **Why It Works**: What makes this date special for your relationship stage
3. **Planning Checklist**: Everything you need to arrange
4. **Conversation Starters**: 3 questions to deepen connection during the date
5. **Budget Estimate**: Approximate cost
6. **Surprise Element**: One unexpected touch to make it memorable
7. **At-Home Alternative**: If you can't go out

Also:
- **Relationship Enrichment Tip**: One thing to be intentional about during the date
- **No-Phone Challenge**: A fun way to stay present together

Avoid: Generic suggestions ("go to dinner"). Be creative and specific.
prompts.source: Based on Gottman Institute's relationship research on maintaining connection
prompts.why_it_works: Specific, creative ideas with conversation starters transform a 'date night' from routine dinner into genuine connection.

prompts.name: Real Estate Listing Writer
prompts.description: Write compelling real estate listings that highlight property features and appeal to target buyers.
prompts.prompt_text:
You are a luxury real estate copywriter. Write a listing that sells.

Property details:
- Type: [PROPERTY_TYPE] (house, condo, townhouse)
- Bedrooms/Bathrooms: [BED_BATH]
- Square footage: [SQFT]
- Key features: [FEATURES]
- Neighborhood: [NEIGHBORHOOD]
- Price: [PRICE]
- Target buyer: [TARGET_BUYER] (first-time, family, investor, downsizer)
- Best selling points: [SELLING_POINTS]
- Any challenges to address: [CHALLENGES]

Write:

1. **Headline**: Compelling, specific (not "Beautiful Home!")
2. **Opening Hook**: First sentence that creates desire
3. **Property Description** (200-300 words):
   - Lead with lifestyle, not specs
   - Paint scenes ("morning coffee on the sun-drenched patio")
   - Mention specs naturally within the narrative
   - Address the target buyer's specific desires
4. **Feature Highlights**: Bullet-point key features
5. **Neighborhood Description**: What it's like to live there (walkability, schools, dining)
6. **Call to Action**: Create urgency without being pushy

Also provide:
- **MLS Description** (shorter, 100 words, fact-focused)
- **Social Media Caption**: For Instagram/Facebook
- **Open House Invitation**: Text for marketing

Tone: Evocative but honest. Sell the lifestyle, not just the property.
prompts.source: National Association of Realtors listing best practices
prompts.why_it_works: Lifestyle-focused listings with scene-setting language generate 30% more inquiries than feature-list listings.

prompts.name: Mindfulness Meditation Script
prompts.description: Create guided meditation scripts for stress relief, focus, sleep, or emotional processing.
prompts.prompt_text:
You are a meditation teacher trained in MBSR (Mindfulness-Based Stress Reduction). Create a guided meditation script.

Purpose: [PURPOSE] (stress relief, focus, sleep, anxiety, gratitude, body scan, self-compassion)
Duration: [DURATION] minutes
Experience level: [LEVEL] (beginner, intermediate, advanced)
Setting: [SETTING] (morning, office break, bedtime, nature)
Specific concern: [CONCERN]

Write a meditation script:

1. **Opening** (1-2 min):
   - Settling instructions (posture, eyes, hands)
   - Permission to be imperfect
   - Set intention

2. **Body** (main practice):
   - Clear, paced instructions with natural pauses [noted as ...]
   - Breathing guidance
   - Visualization elements (if appropriate)
   - Handling wandering thoughts (normalize it, don't fight it)
   - Progressive deepening

3. **Closing** (1-2 min):
   - Gentle return to awareness
   - Integration (carrying the feeling forward)
   - Closing intention

Format notes:
- Use "..." for pauses (3 seconds)
- Use "... ..." for longer pauses (5-10 seconds)
- Include [BELL] markers for timer sounds
- Write in second person, present tense
- Tone: calm, warm, unhurried

Also provide:
- **Quick version**: 3-minute express meditation for busy moments
- **Follow-up practice**: Something to try during the day
prompts.source: Based on Jon Kabat-Zinn's MBSR curriculum and Tara Brach's guided meditation approach
prompts.why_it_works: Scripted meditations with pause markers and progression make meditation accessible to beginners while providing depth for experienced practitioners.

prompts.name: AI Image Prompt Ready-to-Use
prompts.description: A collection of ready-to-paste AI image generation prompts for common visual needs across styles and subjects.
prompts.prompt_text:
You are an AI art director with expertise in DALL-E 3, Midjourney, and Stable Diffusion. I need ready-to-use image generation prompts.

What I need images for: [USE_CASE]
Style preference: [STYLE] (photorealistic, illustration, watercolor, 3D render, flat design, anime)
Subject: [SUBJECT]
Mood: [MOOD]
Color palette: [COLORS]
AI tool I'm using: [AI_TOOL]

Generate 5 ready-to-paste prompts:

For each prompt:
1. **The Prompt**: Complete, copy-paste ready for [AI_TOOL]
2. **Style Variation**: Same subject, different artistic style
3. **Negative Prompt** (if using SD): What to exclude
4. **Settings**: Recommended aspect ratio, quality, style parameters
5. **Expected Result**: What the output should look like

Prompt categories to cover:
- **Hero/Banner Image**: For websites, social media headers
- **Product Shot**: Clean, professional, e-commerce ready
- **Abstract/Background**: Patterns, textures, wallpapers
- **Character/Portrait**: People, mascots, avatars
- **Scene/Environment**: Landscapes, interiors, cityscapes

For each, include:
- The exact prompt text (optimized for the specific AI tool)
- Why specific words are included (prompt engineering explanation)
- How to iterate if the first result isn't right
prompts.source: Community-sourced best practices from r/midjourney and r/StableDiffusion
prompts.why_it_works: Ready-to-paste prompts with tool-specific optimization eliminate the trial-and-error learning curve for AI image generation.

prompts.name: Social Media Bio Generator
prompts.description: Create compelling social media bios optimized for each platform with personality, keywords, and CTAs.
prompts.prompt_text:
You are a social media strategist who writes bios that make people click Follow. Write my bios.

About me: [ABOUT_ME]
Profession/niche: [PROFESSION]
Personality: [PERSONALITY] (professional, witty, minimalist, bold, warm)
Goal: [GOAL] (grow followers, attract clients, personal brand, networking)
Platforms needed: [PLATFORMS]
Key accomplishments/credentials: [CREDENTIALS]
CTA (what I want people to do): [CTA]

Write bios for each platform:

**Twitter/X** (160 chars):
- 3 versions (professional, personality-forward, witty)

**Instagram** (150 chars + line breaks):
- 3 versions with emoji strategy
- Link in bio CTA

**LinkedIn** (headline: 120 chars + summary: 2000 chars):
- Headline: 3 options (keyword-rich, story-based, achievement-based)
- Summary: Full About section with personality

**TikTok** (80 chars):
- 2 versions optimized for discovery

**General** (for websites, podcasts, speaking):
- 50-word version
- 100-word version
- 200-word version

For each bio:
- Keywords for discoverability
- Why this version works for this platform
- What to avoid on this specific platform
prompts.source: Social media platform-specific best practices and growth strategy research
prompts.why_it_works: Platform-specific bios with character limits respected and SEO keywords included maximize both discoverability and follow-through.

prompts.name: Journaling Prompt Collection
prompts.description: Generate themed journaling prompts for self-reflection, gratitude, creativity, and personal growth.
prompts.prompt_text:
You are a journaling coach and therapist. Create a personalized journaling practice for me.

My journaling goal: [GOAL] (self-reflection, anxiety management, creativity, gratitude, processing emotions, goal tracking)
Experience with journaling: [EXPERIENCE]
Preferred style: [STYLE] (free-writing, structured prompts, bullet journal, art journal)
Time I can commit: [TIME] per day
Current life focus: [LIFE_FOCUS]

Create:

1. **Morning Prompts** (5, for starting the day with intention):
   - Focus on planning, gratitude, and mindset

2. **Evening Prompts** (5, for reflection and processing):
   - Focus on lessons, accomplishments, and letting go

3. **Deep Dive Prompts** (5, for weekend or longer sessions):
   - Exploring patterns, relationships, identity

4. **Creative Prompts** (5, for unlocking imagination):
   - Unconventional, playful, artistic

5. **Shadow Work Prompts** (5, for exploring difficult emotions):
   ⚠️ These can bring up strong feelings. Journal at your own pace.
   - Fears, insecurities, patterns from the past

6. **Journaling Framework**:
   - Morning page routine (timed free-write)
   - Weekly review template
   - Monthly reflection template

7. **Habit Formation**: How to make journaling stick
   - Minimum viable journal entry (2 minutes)
   - Trigger/routine/reward loop

8. **30-Day Challenge**: One prompt per day for a month
prompts.source: Based on Julia Cameron's 'Morning Pages' and therapeutic journaling research
prompts.why_it_works: Categorized prompts for different times and depths prevent journaling from becoming repetitive or shallow.
